title: Allocation Formulas, Overflow, and Capacity Proofs · Pointer Forensics
summary: An incident responder inherits a corrupted overflow-safe grid allocator, the earliest failing input, and two plausible hypotheses; they must preserve first-failure evidence, repair the cause, and add a regression. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in overflow-safe grid allocator and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prove count times element-size representability before calling allocation functions.
Check header, padding, alignment, terminator, and payload additions in a safe order.
Characterize whether the target calloc detects product overflow while retaining caller-side policy and tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Capacity Model Proof
steps.instruction:
Order the Capacity Model Proof evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Capacity Model Proof.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Capacity Model Proof assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Capacity Model Proof, keep Capacity Model Proof connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.text: A debug native-systems team handles memory case 5520. Produce bounded C evidence to prove a header-plus-payload allocation formula; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive maximum capacity from object limit, element size, metadata, and stakeholder workload before allocation.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Capacity Model Proof has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Order the regression proof · Debug Capacity Model Proof, name the size, bounds, object, lifetime, owner, borrow, allocation, cleanup, representation, ABI, thread, and transfer boundary, Capacity Model Proof precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Produce bounded C evidence to prove a header-plus-payload allocation formula; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive maximum capacity from object limit, element size, metadata, and stakeholder workload before allocation.

For Order the regression proof · Debug Capacity Model Proof, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: If the arithmetic fits size_t, the requested object is operationally acceptable.
